genePanels.advantages: Gene Panel Advantages: Targeted, cost-effective, short testing cycle. Suitable for genetic diseases with clear clinical suspicion, enabling rapid identification of relevant gene variants.

hero.title: GLSofort® AI Genomic Analysis & Assisted Interpretation Platform

intro.title: GLSofort® Genomic Analysis & Assisted Interpretation Platform
intro.description: GLSofort® connects FASTQ raw data and VCF variant calling with annotation, screening, evidence organization, and report-draft workflows for whole-genome (WGS), whole-exome (WES), and targeted Gene Panel projects. Platform output assists research and clinical analysis and requires review against sample quality, project scope, clinical information, and applicable guidance.
intro.imageCaption: Example of the GLSofort® analysis interface. Fields, data, and functionality vary by project configuration and software version and do not represent an individual test result.

Interpretation depends on data quality and available evidence

Cancer- and inherited-disease-related whole-genome data
Interpretation depends on data quality and available evidence

aiCapabilities.title: An AI-enabled closed-loop analysis workflow
aiCapabilities.description: Bring compute, bioinformatics, structured knowledge, and clinical phenotype information into one analysis chain to reduce tool switching and preserve traceable data and evidence for professional review.

items.title: GPU-Accelerated Secondary Analysis
items.description: Parallel computing supports FASTQ-to-VCF variant calling and can reduce WGS and WES processing wait times. Actual timing depends on data volume, hardware, and analysis parameters.

items.title: Multidimensional Annotation & Local Knowledge
items.description: Automate VCF annotation and combine population frequency, pathogenic-variant, and authorized institutional data according to project configuration for local retrieval and evidence updates.

items.title: AI Screening & HPO Matching
items.description: Assist extraction of HPO terms from clinical descriptions and use machine-learning methods to prioritize candidate genes and variants as structured leads for rare-disease and inherited-disease analysis.

items.title: Reporting & Genetic Counseling Collaboration
items.description: Generate structured preliminary report drafts using project templates and ACMG-related evidence frameworks so clinicians and genetic counselors can review evidence, document judgment, and complete professional review.

aiCapabilities.note: GLSofort® provides analysis and decision support and does not replace qualified professional judgment. Candidate ranking, HPO matching, and report drafts require review against clinical information, validation results, and applicable standards.

page.title: Universal Sequencing Reaction Kit
page.summary: To support broader access to high-throughput sequencing, GenomeLight developed a universal sequencing reaction kit for NextSeq 500/550. The product comprises sequencing reagents, sequencing buffer, sequencing chips, and library diluent and has obtained a Class I medical-device filing in China. Intended use, scope, and performance remain subject to the filing certificate, product instructions, and batch QC documentation.
page.imageAlt: Universal sequencing reaction kit product package
page.imageCaption: Product image from the supplied brochure; packaging and labels are subject to the delivered batch.

items.title: Stable
items.description: The brochure states that defined quality control supports stable sequencing. Refer to the batch quality report and actual run data for batch-specific performance.

items.title: Flexible
items.description: 130M and 400M chip-output options with 50-, 75-, and 300-cycle configurations support different data-volume and read-length needs.

items.title: Fast
items.description: The supplied brochure lists an 8-hour sequencing time for the 130M, 50-cycle configuration. Other configurations are shown in the performance table.

items.title: Accurate
items.description: The brochure states sequencing accuracy above 99.9%. Actual sequencing quality should be assessed from run data and batch quality-control results.

performance.title: Product Performance
performance.note: These are stated values from the supplied brochure. Actual performance may vary with instrument status, library type and quality, insert size, loading concentration, and laboratory workflow. Refer to the product instructions, filing certificate, and batch quality report.

applications.title: Applications
applications.note: Samples/run values are typical configurations listed in the supplied brochure and are not fixed commitments for every laboratory or clinical project. Confirm the workflow against sample quality, target data volume, laboratory validation, and applicable requirements.

cta.title: Request product instructions, batch QC, and purchasing information
cta.description: Contact the GenomeLight product team to confirm compatible configurations, filing documents, delivery specifications, or batch test information.

development.title: Developed to help manage the total cost of NGS workflows
development.description: GenomeLight develops universal sequencing reagent kits around compatibility, throughput configuration, and quality-control needs on mainstream sequencing platforms. The product gives hospitals, research teams, and independent clinical laboratories an additional option alongside original-platform reagents. Instrument status, library type, laboratory validation, and applicable requirements must be evaluated before implementation.

items.title: Platform-Oriented Design
items.description: Developed for NextSeq 500/550 workflows with sequencing reagents, buffer, chips, and library diluent supplied as a coordinated configuration.

items.title: Performance Evidence
items.description: The brochure lists nominal 130M/400M chip output, Q30, and sequencing-time metrics. Interpret performance against the selected configuration, run data, and batch QC.

items.title: Multiple Workflow Scenarios
items.description: Can be evaluated for NIPT, pathogen metagenomic sequencing, and tumor-related targeted sequencing workflows, subject to validation by the implementing laboratory.

development.note: The Class I medical-device filing number is 渝高新械备20250033. The filing does not constitute one performance commitment for every library, instrument condition, or clinical use. Confirm the filing certificate and product instructions before use.

hero.title: Health Screening
hero.subtitle: Genetic risk association assessment and personalized health management information; results are not a diagnosis, prediction, or treatment recommendation

overview.title: Health Screening Services Overview
overview.description: Services cover selected genetic risks, carrier status, and pharmacogenomic information for children and adults. Results describe associations and require interpretation with family history, lifestyle, and clinical information.

healthManagement.title: Age-Appropriate Health Information
healthManagement.description: Genetic risk association and health management information for children and adults, within each project's stated scope.

geneticTesting.title: Genetic Information Analysis
geneticTesting.description: Assesses associations between selected genetic factors and health risks; it is not diagnosis or deterministic prediction.

medicationInstruction.title: Medication-Related Genetic Information
medicationInstruction.description: Provides pharmacogenomic information. A physician must decide prescriptions, doses, and medication changes.

adult.title: Adult Whole Exome Health Assessment Package
adult.description: Assesses selected inherited-disease risks, carrier status, and pharmacogenomic variants. Results present probabilities and evidence levels, do not provide a diagnosis or treatment decision, and require professional interpretation with clinical information.

child.title: Pediatric Whole Exome Health Assessment Package
child.description: Provides information about selected genetic risks and carrier status in children. Testing requires informed guardian involvement and does not replace pediatric assessment or routine care.

adult.title: Adult Whole Genome Health Assessment Package
adult.description: Analyzes selected variants across the whole genome for genetic risk, carrier status, and pharmacogenomic information. Interpretation depends on data quality, available evidence, and knowledge-base updates.

child.title: Pediatric Whole Genome Health Assessment Package
child.description: Analyzes selected variants across a child's whole genome. Results reflect current genetic evidence and cannot predict ability, achievement, or future health with certainty.

adult.title: Adult Gene Chip Screening Package
adult.description: Analyzes more than 700,000 sequence sites according to project configuration, with information that may cover risk associations, pharmacogenomics, diet, exercise, nutrition, and traits. Refer to the report for included sites and evidence levels.

child.title: Pediatric Gene Chip Screening Package
child.description: Gene chip screening designed specifically for children, covering multiple dimensions of genetic testing including growth and development, nutritional metabolism, and safe medication.

cancer.title: Male and Female Cancer Seven-Item Panel
cancer.description: Assesses selected inherited susceptibility factors for common cancers to support discussion of screening with a professional; it does not replace routine or clinical screening.

childMedicine.title: Pediatric Safe Medication (115 Items)
childMedicine.description: Uses sources such as CPIC guidance and PharmGKB to provide pediatric pharmacogenomic information. A physician must consider age, weight, condition, and other medicines when prescribing.

healthReport.title: Personal Health Check-up 78-Item Genetic Testing Report
healthReport.description: Analyzes selected genetic factors associated with common health concerns as a reference for health-management discussions. Lifestyle, diet, and screening decisions require appropriate professional advice.

childTraits.title: Pediatric Growth Traits Genetic Testing Product
childTraits.description: Presents selected genetic associations related to growth traits. These traits are influenced by genetics, environment, education, and other factors; results must not be used to judge a child's potential or ability.

comprehensive.title: Comprehensive Coverage
comprehensive.description: From children to adults, from disease risks to personality traits, providing full life-cycle genetic health management services.

precision.title: Evidence-Based Analysis
precision.description: Uses published research and databases for risk-association analysis; evidence level and applicability are described in the report.

safety.title: Safe Medication
safety.description: Provides pharmacogenomic information for physician review and must not be used to stop, switch, or change the dose of medication without medical advice.

lifelong.title: Updated Interpretation
lifelong.description: With authorization and available data, selected interpretations may be updated as research and databases evolve; scope and timing follow the service agreement.

article.title: Clinical Phenotype-to-HPO Conversion: GLSofort® AI Update
article.lead: The updated GLSofort® converts clinical language into standard HPO terms and organizes associated diseases, candidate genes, and phenotype-match information for a more efficient and consistent genetics-analysis entry point.

images.workflowAlt: GLSofort AI interface extracting HPO terms from clinical notes
images.workflowCaption: GLSofort® HPO (AI) demonstration: standard HPO terms are extracted from a complete clinical description and can be copied into the next analysis step.
images.geneAlt: GLSofort HPO-associated gene ranking and phenotype matching interface
images.geneCaption: The associated-gene view brings together candidate genes, phenotype match scores, and mapped HPO terms for further review.

upgrades.title: Moving from keyword matching to clinical semantics
upgrades.description: The update addresses three key stages of phenotype normalization and reduces repetitive work involved in looking up HPO terms one by one.

items.title: Semantic understanding
items.description: Multi-model capabilities interpret clinical descriptions beyond preset keywords and map varied or conversational wording to standard HPO terms.

items.title: Batch extraction
items.description: A complete clinical note can be processed at once to identify phenotype entities and return the relevant HPO codes without repeated entry.

items.title: Associated evidence
items.description: Alongside HPO terms, the workflow organizes related diseases, candidate genes, and phenotype-match information as leads for professional analysis.

workflow.title: From clinical notes to structured analysis leads
workflow.description: Phenotype text, HPO normalization, and associated information can be reviewed in one workflow before moving into downstream analysis.
steps: Enter the patient's clinical findings or a complete clinical note.
Run the HPO (AI) tool to identify and normalize phenotypes.
Review HPO results, associated genes, and phenotype-match information.
Copy or download the results for downstream variant analysis and professional review.

case.title: Organizing a complex multisystem phenotype
case.description: The source article demonstrates a case involving recurrent fractures, blue sclera, dental wear, hearing loss, and other multisystem findings. The tool gathers the related HPO terms and presents candidate genes with match information, helping analysts structure leads from a long clinical description.
case.note: The example demonstrates the product workflow. Outputs must still be reviewed against the source record, laboratory data, applicable guidance, and qualified professional judgment.

value.title: What the update changes in the workflow
value.description: The role of AI is to bring normalization, retrieval, and evidence organization into a reviewable state sooner, not to replace professional judgment.

items.title: Efficiency
items.description: In the source demonstration, phenotype organization moved from roughly 10-15 minutes to about 30 seconds. Actual time depends on text length, system configuration, and review requirements.

items.title: Consistency
items.description: Different clinical writing styles can be mapped to the standard HPO framework, reducing the effect of wording variation during retrieval.

items.title: Reviewability
items.description: HPO terms, candidate genes, and match information appear together so analysts can verify each item, add evidence, and document their reasoning.

responsible.title: Scope of assisted analysis
responsible.description: GLSofort® supports data organization and assisted analysis; it does not independently provide a medical diagnosis or treatment recommendation. Qualified professionals must review outputs with clinical information, test quality, and applicable requirements.

cta.title: Explore GLSofort® AI-assisted analysis
cta.description: Contact the team to discuss HPO conversion and a workflow suitable for your project.

companyIntro.title: Company Introduction
companyIntro.description1: Located in Chongqing Hi-tech Development Zone, we are a high-tech life health enterprise driven by precision medical services and AI big data technology, innovating future medical models.
companyIntro.description2: GenomeLight focuses on interpretation, annotation, and analysis of next-generation sequencing (NGS) data across whole-genome (WGS), whole-exome (WES), and targeted Gene Panel workflows. GLSofort® combines GPU acceleration, knowledge bases, and AI-assisted phenotype matching from raw data to report drafts. To help reduce the overall cost of NGS workflows, the company also develops universal sequencing reagent kits for relevant platforms and has obtained a Class I medical-device filing in China. Products and services support healthcare organizations, research teams, and personal health contexts; qualified professionals must review outputs within the project scope and relevant clinical information.

facts.title: Founded in 2021
facts.description: Building product capabilities across sequencing, analysis, and laboratory digitalization.

facts.title: Chongqing / Shenzhen
facts.description: Coordinated product development, project implementation, and customer support.

facts.title: Integrated NGS
facts.description: Sequencing reagents, data analysis, professional review, and laboratory management.

mission.title: Mission
mission.content: Work closely with the medical community to provide careful data-analysis support for professional judgment.

vision.title: Vision
vision.content: Use data-analysis technology to support inherited-disease healthcare and research needs.

product.title: Product Philosophy
product.content: Technology innovation and technical leadership in high-quality products and services are the foundation of our enterprise and the direct embodiment of technological innovation. GenomeLight will focus on solving industry pain points.

service.title: Service Philosophy
service.content: Integrity first, customer paramount. GenomeLight will provide the highest quality service with a professional, focused, and dedicated attitude.

values.title: Core Values
values.content: We continuously seek better solutions to meet the needs of patients and healthcare providers. We deeply understand patients and medical professionals, caring for and respecting their feelings. We adhere to the highest ethical and transparency standards, ensuring data security and confidentiality. We actively collaborate with the medical community, research institutions, and patient communities to jointly improve genetic disease management.

team.title: Team Introduction
team.description: The team spans medical genetics, clinical molecular genetics, bioinformatics, AI, laboratory workflow, and implementation. Automated analysis and system tools enter real workflows through defined quality-control and professional-review checkpoints.

liWenhui.name: Laura Li, PhD
liWenhui.title: FACMGG, CTO
liWenhui.description: PhD in molecular biology and genetics from Johns Hopkins University and postdoctoral training at Caltech, with more than 30 years of experience in cell biology, genomics, and clinical molecular genetics. Her work has included clinical laboratory leadership and genomic research in pediatric genetic disorders and cancer.

experience.title: Professional Experience

Former associate director of molecular pathology and genetics at Children's Hospital Los Angeles
Long-standing work across whole-genome, Gene Panel, and clinical molecular genetics workflows

achievements.title: Areas of Practice

privacy.title: Privacy
privacy.content: We treat genomic data and sensitive health information with care. Depending on the contract and deployment, projects may use access controls, encryption in transit, separation of identifiers, and data-retention policies. Applicable laws, industry requirements, data location, and user permissions must be confirmed before implementation.

hero.title: LIMS Laboratory Information Management System
hero.subtitle: Designed for sequencing laboratories, hospital testing departments, and medical laboratories, connecting samples, tasks, SOPs, experimental data, QC review, and report release in one traceable workflow.

workflow.title: Trace every sample from receipt to archive
workflow.description: LIMS is more than an electronic notebook. It organizes tasks, data, and responsibility records around real laboratory operations so progress, exceptions, reviews, and deliverables remain visible in one workflow.

steps.title: Sample receipt
steps.description: Register samples, projects, and customer information with a unique identifier and receipt record.

steps.title: Tasks and SOPs
steps.description: Assign tasks by project type and connect the applicable SOP, batch requirements, and responsible staff.

steps.title: Experiment and data
steps.description: Record experimental steps, instrument runs, and key data while reducing repeated manual transcription.

steps.title: Quality review
steps.description: Review QC checkpoints, exception handling, comments, and approval status in one place.

steps.title: Report release
steps.description: Generate, review, and release reports through an approval workflow with version and responsibility records.

steps.title: Archive and traceability
steps.description: Connect samples, tasks, data, reports, and audit logs for later retrieval and review.

capabilities.title: Quality systems, efficiency, and interoperability
capabilities.description: Bring information from spreadsheets, paper records, and isolated instruments into a unified view for daily operations, quality management, and assessment preparation.

items.title: Quality and audit records
items.description: Support SOP versions, activity records, reviews, and audit logs that help laboratories build evidence around ISO 15189, CAP, CLIA, and related quality-system requirements.

items.title: Proficiency testing
items.description: Track proficiency-testing tasks with reminders, status, and result archives to reduce missed steps and retain the full handling history.

items.title: Less duplicate entry
items.description: Use workflow automation, instrument data collection, and report templates to reduce repeated entry. Actual efficiency depends on interfaces, configuration, and implementation scope.

items.title: System interoperability
items.description: Standard interfaces and project-specific adaptation can support HIS, LIS, PACS, OA, and instrument connections, subject to the implementation plan.

capabilities.note: LIMS supports quality management and process traceability; it does not automatically confer certification. Certification depends on laboratory policies, staff execution, method validation, documentation, and assessment requirements.

plans.title: Three configurations for different operating stages
plans.description: From the core laboratory workflow to integrated operations and hospital-wide connectivity, scope can be selected according to laboratory size, existing systems, interfaces, and data-security requirements.

items.label: CORE WORKFLOW
items.title: Mini
items.description: Focused on laboratory workflow management for organizations that first need to close the loop across samples, SOPs, data, and reports.

items.label: INTEGRATED OPERATIONS
items.title: Standard
items.description: Adds MiniERP to the Mini configuration, extending laboratory workflow into materials, equipment, staff, and cost management.

items.label: HOSPITAL ECOSYSTEM
items.title: Premium
items.description: For large hospitals and laboratory groups requiring hospital-system integration, intranet deployment, and controlled data exchange.
features: HIS, LIS, PACS, and OA interfaces

operations.title: Extend from laboratory workflow to operations
operations.description: The Standard and Premium configurations can combine MiniERP modules with laboratory tasks so materials, equipment, staff, and cost information do not sit apart from operational work.

items.title: Reagents and consumables
items.description: Manage receipt, issue, lot, expiry, and inventory status, and connect records to laboratory tasks.

items.title: Instruments and equipment
items.description: Record equipment status, maintenance schedules, calibration information, and use.

items.title: People and tasks
items.description: View workload, progress, and role responsibility to support scheduling and collaboration.

items.title: Cost and analysis
items.description: Aggregate materials, tasks, and operational records as structured inputs for costing and management analysis.

deployment.title: Fit existing systems and hospital networks
deployment.description: Use interfaces, permissions, and deployment strategies to embed LIMS into the current laboratory environment. Interface availability, synchronization, and data scope should be confirmed before implementation.
deployment.imageAlt: GenomeLight LIMS workflow connecting MiniERP, HIS, LIS, OA, ERP, and laboratory operations
deployment.imageCaption: The GenomeLight LIMS workflow published in the product announcement, showing the relationship between core LIMS, MiniERP, and external systems.

items.title: Standard interfaces
items.description: Project-based integration can support HIS, LIS, PACS, OA, ERP, and instruments to reduce repeated import and export.

items.title: Flexible deployment
items.description: On-premise, isolated intranet, and other deployment options can be evaluated against network and data-security requirements.

items.title: Permissions and audit trail
items.description: Role permissions, approval checkpoints, and activity logs support access control and process traceability.

items.title: Which laboratories should consider LIMS?
items.description: Sequencing laboratories, hospital testing departments, and medical laboratories that manage samples, SOPs, experimental data, QC, and reports can evaluate LIMS. Configuration depends on scale, workflow, interfaces, and quality-system requirements.

items.title: Can it connect to existing instruments and hospital systems?
items.description: Standard interfaces and adaptation can support instruments as well as HIS, LIS, PACS, and OA. Direct connectivity depends on the other system interface, protocol, network, and authorization conditions.

items.title: Does deploying LIMS automatically satisfy certification requirements?
items.description: No. LIMS can support SOPs, records, reviews, permissions, and audit logs, but certification still depends on the laboratory documentation system, staff execution, method validation, continual improvement, and formal assessment.

cta.title: Plan your laboratory digital transformation
cta.description: Contact the team to evaluate a LIMS configuration against your current workflow, interfaces, and quality-management requirements.

dataAssets.title: Turn local data into searchable, analyzable laboratory assets
dataAssets.description: Within authorization and deployment boundaries, LIMS can organize samples, workflows, results, versions, and activity records as structured data for historical search, local statistics, and quality review.

items.title: Localized Data
items.description: Store authorized data and records in the organization's environment according to the deployment plan, with location and retention agreed by the project.

items.title: Local Detection Frequency
items.description: Connect with analysis systems to support authorized statistics on local detection frequency and historical records.

items.title: Historical Traceability
items.description: Link samples, workflows, results, and versions for permission-based retrieval, review, and audit.

items.title: Visualization & Reports
items.description: Generate statistical views and reports from structured data, with metrics configured to laboratory requirements.

dataAssets.note: Local statistics support laboratory operations, quality management, and authorized research. They should not be interpreted without considering sample quality, methodology, and population context.
